subject: Geology
title: Foliation
description: A short lesson to help you apply Foliation.

hook.text: Some metamorphic rocks split into thin, flat sheets. That layered look has a name: foliation.

buildup.text: Foliation forms when pressure forces platy minerals like mica to align perpendicular to the stress direction.

discovery.text: Slate's smooth cleavage, schist's wavy sheets, and gneiss's bold bands are all different types of foliation.

twist.text: Non-foliated rocks like marble and quartzite have equant minerals that can't align, so they lack layering.

climax.text: Foliation records the direction of ancient stress. It's a compass pointing to past tectonic forces.

quiz.question: What causes foliation in metamorphic rocks?
options: Pressure forcing platy minerals to align
